meta_note: All routes are prefixed with /api/v1/auth. Authentication tokens are JWT-based. See conventions below.

conventions.refresh_token: Passed in the request body as refreshToken
conventions.otp_token: x-token: <token>  — short-lived OTP-scoped JWT returned by register or verify-password-token
conventions.two_factor_token: Passed in the request body as twoFactorToken — short-lived JWT (5m) returned by login when 2FA is required

auth_register.description: Register a new user with email and password. Returns the user record and a short-lived emailToken (otp_token) to pass as x-token when calling /auth/verify.

input.password: string (required) — min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 symbol

data.emailToken: string — otp_token JWT; pass as x-token to POST /auth/verify

responses.400: Validation error — missing or invalid fields

auth_verifyEmail.description: Verify the user's email using the OTP sent to their inbox. Requires the emailToken from /auth/register as the x-token header. Creates the first session and returns access + refresh tokens.

input.otp: string (required) — 6-digit code from the verification email

responses.401: OTP invalid / expired, or x-token missing / invalid

auth_resendVerifyEmail.description: Resend the email verification OTP. Deletes the previous pending token and issues a new emailToken.

responses.409: User already verified, or a valid token still exists and has not expired

auth_login.description: Authenticate with email and password. Returns access + refresh tokens on success. If the account has 2FA enabled, returns a twoFactorToken and requiresTwoFactor:true instead — the client must then call POST /auth/2fa/verify.

responses.200: Login successful — tokens or 2FA challenge returned
responses.401: Invalid credentials or account locked after too many failed attempts

auth_forgotPassword.description: Initiate a password reset. Dispatches a 6-digit OTP to the user's email. Always returns 200 to prevent user enumeration.

data.email: string — echoed back, or empty string if user not found

responses.200: Always 200 regardless of whether the email exists

auth_resendForgotPassword.description: Resend the password reset OTP after the previous one has expired.

responses.409: A valid reset token already exists and has not yet expired

auth_verifyPasswordToken.description: Verify the 6-digit OTP from the forgot-password email. Returns a passwordToken (otp_token JWT) to pass as x-token when calling /auth/reset-password.

data.passwordToken: string — otp_token JWT; pass as x-token to POST /auth/reset-password

auth_resetPassword.description: Set a new password using the passwordToken from /auth/verify-password-token. Pass the token as the x-token header.

input.newPassword: string (required) — min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 symbol

responses.409: New password is the same as the current password

auth_refreshToken.description: Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair. The old refresh token is invalidated (rotation).
auth_refreshToken.authentication: None — refreshToken passed in request body

responses.401: Refresh token invalid, expired, or session not found

auth_loginWithGoogle.description: Sign in or register using a Google id_token from the client-side OAuth flow. The server verifies the token against Google's public keys. Creates or signs in the user and returns access + refresh tokens.

responses.401: Google id_token invalid, expired, or signature mismatch

auth_init2fa.description: Generate a TOTP secret and otpauth URI for the authenticated user. Render the URI as a QR code for the user to scan. 2FA is not yet active until /auth/2fa/confirm succeeds.

data.secret: string — base32 TOTP secret (show as fallback for manual entry)
data.otpauthUri: string — otpauth://totp/... URI for QR code

auth_confirm2fa.description: Confirm 2FA setup by verifying the first TOTP code from the authenticator app. Activates 2FA and returns 10 one-time backup codes — shown only once; the client must display them immediately.

responses.401: Access token invalid, or TOTP code incorrect / expired

auth_verify2fa.description: Complete a 2FA login challenge with a TOTP code or backup code. Rate limited to 5 attempts per 15 minutes to prevent brute-force attacks. Backup codes are single-use.
auth_verify2fa.authentication: None — identity proven via twoFactorToken in the body

input.twoFactorToken: string (required) — short-lived JWT from POST /auth/login when 2FA required
input.code: string (required) — 6-digit TOTP code or 8-character backup code (min length 6)

responses.401: twoFactorToken invalid / expired, or TOTP / backup code incorrect
responses.429: Too many attempts — rate limit exceeded (5 per 15 minutes)

auth_disable2fa.description: Disable TOTP 2FA on the account. Requires the current TOTP code. Wipes the stored secret and all backup codes.

responses.401: Access token invalid or TOTP code incorrect

auth_changePassword.description: Change the authenticated user's password. Verifies the current password first. Drops all active sessions on success and sends a security alert email.

input.newPassword: string (required) — min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 symbol

responses.401: Access token invalid or current password incorrect
responses.409: New password is identical to the current password

auth_initiateEmailChange.description: Begin an email change. Verifies password ownership, checks the new email is available, and dispatches a 6-digit OTP to the new address. Submit the OTP to /auth/email/verify to complete the change.

input.newEmail: string (required) — valid email address; stored lowercase

output.message: OTP sent to new email address

data.newEmail: string — the address the OTP was dispatched to

responses.200: OTP dispatched to the new email address
responses.401: Access token invalid or current password incorrect
responses.409: New email is already registered to another account

auth_verifyEmailChange.description: Complete an email change by submitting the OTP sent to the new address. Updates the email, deletes the pending token, drops all sessions, and sends a security alert to the old address.

input.otp: string (required) — exactly 6-digit code sent to the new email

responses.401: Access token invalid, or OTP incorrect / expired
responses.409: Target email was claimed by another account between initiation and verification
